name: p1v2-typed-verdict-lesson-boundary
purpose: Compile recorded operational verdicts into evidence-bound typed lessons and prepare equal-token four-arm prompts without owning durable or scientific judgment state.
rationale: The existing generic-feedback-runtime already owns durable replay, role separation, and append authority.
Typed lesson semantics currently have one executable consumer and no independent scheduling, recovery, or mutable-state authority.
A pure module keeps model, tokenizer, environment, persistence, and scientific judge behind explicit callers or ports.

boundary.owns: Strict typed-lesson compilation, sealed-cut admission, deterministic scope retrieval, exact prompt-token parity verification, and verdict-free L0 observations
boundary.mechanism: Transform recorded verdict mappings and explicit allow-lists into immutable content-addressed lessons and equal-budget prompt plans
policy_outside: Which LLM produces an operational verdict
Scientific progress thresholds and LakatoTree judgment
non_goals: Network or model calls inside the compiler
boundary.state_authority: The module owns no mutable state; callers or generic-feedback-runtime own recorded inputs and publication

contract: compile_typed_lesson(recorded_operational_verdict_v1, sealed_cut_policy_v1) -> TypedLessonV1 | LessonContractError; retrieve_lessons(query, lessons, top_k) -> LessonSelectionV1; build_prompt_parity_plan(contexts, render, tokenizer_padder_port) -> PromptParityPlanV1 | PromptParityError
invariants: Only frozen training episode and evidence IDs may enter a lesson.
Heldout identifiers, gold-derived strings, evaluation labels, and future episodes fail closed.
Identical verdict, policy, query, lessons, tokenizer, and padding inputs produce identical hashes and selections.
Every arm has the exact same rendered user-prompt token count before model execution.
Measurement records contain observations and provenance but no scientific verdict field.
failure_model: Malformed, extra-field, low-confidence, unknown-evidence, future-ID, gold-text, and unknown-supersession inputs raise typed errors before output.
Tokenizer or padder inability to reach exact parity rejects the plan before any model call.
Answer receipt identity, call-count, or prompt-token mismatch rejects the observation.
verification: Golden determinism and content-address tests for compiler, retrieval, and context rendering.
Injected leakage tests for future episode, heldout task, gold text, extra field, and unknown supersession.
Four-arm exact-token parity and bad-padder refusal tests.
L0 fake-answerer test proving gold is absent from every call and measurement evidence contains no verdict.
promotion_gates: At least two independent runtime consumers require the same mutable lesson lifecycle.
Lesson publication needs durable single-writer admission, recovery, cancellation, or backpressure not supplied by generic-feedback-runtime.
A stable migration contract is required across stored lesson schema versions.
falsifiers: If P1v2 adapters require incompatible lesson semantics, keep separate domain modules rather than promoting a false shared engine.
If exact token parity cannot be reproduced against the deployed tokenizer, L0 and L1 measurement remain forbidden.
If an oracle typed lesson cannot change heldout behavior, stop before compiler learning.
